name: interpark-layout-72b
prompt: You are a layout parser for concert hall seat maps. Analyze the attached seat layout image and extract EVERY section, even small ones. Scan systematically from the stage outward, row by row. Each visually distinct colored block (purple floor block, blue 2F/3F block, green special block, etc.) is one section. Do not merge adjacent same-color blocks — they are separate sections even if they look connected. Extract: 1. The location of the "무대" (Stage). Typically TOP / BOTTOM / LEFT / RIGHT / CENTER. 2. All named seat sections (구역) shown in the map — including 2-digit and 3-digit numeric labels (e.g. "211", "308", "314") and any letter labels where present on the map. Only extract section labels you can clearly read in the image — do not invent or guess. 3. For each section, determine: - "name": the section label as shown on the map (without the "구역" suffix). - "distance_tier": Integer 1-5 (1 = closest to stage, 5 = furthest). - "horizontal_alignment": Integer representing horizontal alignment (0 = center column; negative = left, positive = right). For 3 columns: -1, 0, +1. For 5 columns: -2, -1, 0, +1, +2. - "floor": one of "FLOOR", "2F", "3F". CRITICAL: a printed floor label (e.g. "2F" appearing once near section 215) describes the ENTIRE tier or ring of sections at that visual height — NOT just the single section next to the label. If "2F" appears once but you see ~10 visually-aligned sections at the same elevation, all of them are 2F. Look for the label, then classify every section in the same horizontal ring / vertical band accordingly. Sections at the outermost arc (often labeled "3F" or shown in a separate colour) belong to the next tier out. - "diagonal_tier": Integer 0-3 capturing the radial / corner offset (0 = on the central axis straight back from stage; 3 = extreme corner). For sections directly behind stage set to 0; for sections hugging the side walls set to 1-2; for sections at the very back corners (e.g. 308, 314) set to 3. - "special": optional. Set to "wheelchair" if the section is for wheelchair users (look for the ♿ symbol), "obstructed" if view is blocked by a console or pillar, "partial-view" if part of the stage is cut off. Omit otherwise. Provide the result ONLY as a valid JSON object (no markdown, no comments): { "stage_location": "TOP", "sections": [{ "name": "A", "distance_tier": 1, "horizontal_alignment": 0, "floor": "FLOOR", "diagonal_tier": 0 }, { "name": "211", "distance_tier": 2, "horizontal_alignment": -1, "floor": "3F", "diagonal_tier": 1 }] }
